message.content:
You are verifier #2, independent. Do NOT optimistically confirm. Read the ACTUAL code at /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay and decide if this is a TRUE production-readiness problem in CURRENT code (4 hardening passes applied). Confirmed=true ONLY if the problem exists as described, the PoC reproduces, and it is genuine (not speculative/already-handled/by-design). Cite file:line.

H-001: alpha and stability_threshold are post-hoc free parameters, not pre-registered — threshold HARKing survives the firewall
File: src/assay_engine/methodology/confirm.py:101-143, 292-397
Description: The pre-registration digest (canonical_hypothesis_digest, preregistration.py:114-118) binds statement/kind/origin/test_name/decision_rule/source_claim_id/predicted_direction/params. Verified: `alpha` and `stability_threshold` are NOT fields on Hypothesis (hypothesis.py:61-74) and are NOT in the digest payload. They are supplied as plain runtime arguments to verdict_from_pvalue/confirm_whole_corpus/confirm_unit_level at confirmation time, and verdict_from_pvalue even CONSTRUCTS the recorded decision_rule string from the runtime alpha (confirm.py:131 `rule = f"...alpha={alpha}..."`) instead of comparing it against the locked decision_rule. Nothing reads hypothesis.decision_rule/params to constrain the confirm-time threshold. An analyst who has seen the null/baseline can therefore choose alpha=0.05 vs 0.10 or stability_threshold=0.9 vs 0.6 to flip indeterminate↔supported — exactly the post-hoc-threshold HARKing the firewall claims to prevent. The most decision-bearing numbers are not committed before confirmation, defeating the core methodological guarantee.
PoC: Lock a WHOLE_CORPUS hypothesis with predicted_direction='greater' (alpha not a field, so the threshold is unlocked). After seeing the null: confirm_whole_corpus(h, observed=X, null_distribution=null, alpha=0.05, resample_statistics=R, stability_threshold=0.9, authority=auth) -> indeterminate ('does not beat null at alpha'). Re-run the SAME locked hypothesis (require_preregistered still passes — content unchanged) with alpha=0.10, stability_threshold=0.6 -> supported. Verdict changed purely by post-hoc threshold choice. Same applies to confirm_unit_level's alpha.
Suggested fix: Add `alpha` (and optional `stability_threshold`) as typed fields on Hypothesis, include them in canonical_hypothesis_digest, and have the confirmers READ them from the locked hypothesis instead of accepting caller arguments — or, if kept as arguments, require they equal the locked values and raise PreRegistrationError on mismatch (mirroring _resolve_direction's cross-check). Build the recorded decision_rule from the locked value, not the runtime arg.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

90	
91	def _validate_pvalue(p_value: float) -> None:
92	    if not (0.0 <= p_value <= 1.0):
93	        raise ValueError(f"p_value must be in [0, 1]; got {p_value}")
94	
95	
96	def _validate_finite(name: str, value: float) -> None:
97	    if not math.isfinite(value):
98	        raise ValueError(f"{name} must be a finite number; got {value}")
99	
100	
101	def verdict_from_pvalue(
102	    hypothesis_id: str,
103	    *,
104	    statistic: float,
105	    p_value: float,
106	    alpha: float,
107	    powered: bool,
108	    direction_supports_claim: bool,
109	    test_description: str = "confirmatory test",
110	) -> Verdict:
111	    """Map a confirmatory statistic onto the three verdicts.
112	
113	    - not ``powered`` → ``indeterminate`` (the method cannot decide).
114	    - significant and direction agrees with the claim → ``supported``.
115	    - significant and direction disagrees → ``contradicted``.
116	    - not significant → ``indeterminate`` (absence of evidence is not contradiction here).
117	
118	    ``p_value`` and ``direction_supports_claim`` must be derived by the caller from a test
119	    appropriate to the claim's predicted direction. For a unit-level held-out test the engine
120	    is handed only an opaque ``statistic`` + ``p_value`` and cannot itself infer whether the
121	    statistic supports or contradicts the claim — so ``direction_supports_claim`` is a trusted
122	    caller obligation, ``statistic`` is recorded for provenance only (it does NOT gate the
123	    verdict), and the deciding flag is recorded in ``evidence`` so the verdict is re-derivable
124	    (audit pass 2, issue #20). Inputs are validated (issue #4) and the recorded decision rule
125	    reflects the caller's ``test_description``, not a hard-coded sidedness label (issue #2).
126	    """
127	    _validate_pvalue(p_value)
128	    _validate_alpha(alpha)
129	    _validate_finite("statistic", statistic)
130	
131	    rule = f"{test_description} at alpha={alpha}; powered={powered}"
132	    common = dict(
133	        statistic=statistic,
134	        threshold=alpha,
135	        evidence={"p_value": p_value, "direction_supports_claim": direction_supports_claim},
136	    )
137	    if not powered:
138	        return Verdict.indeterminate(hypothesis_id, rule, notes="underpowered", **common)
139	    if p_value <= alpha:
140	        if direction_supports_claim:
141	            return Verdict.supported(hypothesis_id, rule, **common)
142	        return Verdict.contradicted(hypothesis_id, rule, **common)
143	    return Verdict.indeterminate(hypothesis_id, rule, notes="not significant at alpha", **common)
144	
145	
146	def confirm_unit_level(
147	    hypothesis: Hypothesis,
148	    *,
149	    split: DiscoverConfirmSplit,
150	    evaluated_ids: Iterable[str],
151	    statistic: float,
152	    p_value: float,
153	    alpha: float,
154	    powered: bool,
155	    direction_supports_claim: bool,
156	    authority: TimestampAuthority | None = None,
157	) -> Verdict:
158	    """Confirm a unit-level hypothesis on the held-out partition only.
159	
160	    Pass ``authority`` to verify pre-registration in full (content binding + timestamp +
161	    lock-before-now); omit it to fall back to the presence gate (see :func:`require_locked`).
162	
163	    Direction (pass 4, #G-017): unlike :func:`confirm_whole_corpus` — where the verdict polarity
164	    is derived from the pre-registered ``predicted_direction`` tail — the unit-level verdict is
165	    governed SOLELY by the caller-supplied ``direction_supports_claim`` flag (the engine sees only
166	    an opaque held-out ``statistic``/``p_value`` and cannot itself infer the tail). The
167	    hypothesis's ``predicted_direction`` is therefore advisory at unit level: it is shape-validated
168	    below for safety but does NOT decide the verdict. A study that wants the locked tail to bind the
169	    unit-level decision must derive ``direction_supports_claim`` from it in its own confirmer.
170	    """
171	    if hypothesis.kind is not HypothesisKind.UNIT_LEVEL:
172	        raise ValueError("confirm_unit_level requires a UNIT_LEVEL hypothesis")
173	    # Defense-in-depth direction validation (pass 3, #F-002): the unit-level path takes the
174	    # deciding direction as a trusted caller flag (``direction_supports_claim``) and so never
175	    # routed through ``_resolve_direction`` — which left a locked-but-invalid
176	    # ``predicted_direction`` (e.g. "UP") entirely unchecked here. ``Hypothesis.__post_init__``
177	    # now rejects such values at construction, but re-validate any pre-registered direction here
178	    # so a hypothesis constructed by an unusual path (or a future field mutation) cannot confirm
179	    # against an unrecognized tail.
180	    if hypothesis.predicted_direction is not None and (
181	        hypothesis.predicted_direction not in _VALID_DIRECTIONS
182	    ):
183	        raise ValueError(
184	            f"predicted_direction must be one of {_VALID_DIRECTIONS}; got "
185	            f"{hypothesis.predicted_direction!r}"
186	        )
187	    _gate_preregistration(hypothesis, authority)
188	    split.assert_confirm_only(evaluated_ids)  # Firewall B (rejects empty / discovery ids)
189	    return verdict_from_pvalue(
190	        hypothesis.hypothesis_id,
191	        statistic=statistic,
192	        p_value=p_value,
193	        alpha=alpha,
194	        powered=powered,
195	        direction_supports_claim=direction_supports_claim,
196	        test_description="held-out unit-level test",
197	    )
198	
199	
200	def _empirical_p(null: Sequence[float], observed: float, tail: Direction) -> float:
201	    """One-sided empirical p-value with +1 smoothing for the given tail."""
202	    n = len(null)
203	    if tail == "greater":
204	        extreme = sum(1 for v in null if v >= observed)
205	    else:
206	        extreme = sum(1 for v in null if v <= observed)
207	    return (extreme + 1) / (n + 1)
208	
209

1	"""Pre-registration integrity (METHODOLOGY.md §3, GOVERNANCE.md §2).
2	
3	GOVERNANCE §2 defines pre-registration as three steps: a hypothesis is (1) made specific and
4	typed, (2) **content-hashed and committed**, and (3) **timestamped against a trusted authority**
5	— *before* any confirmatory test. Steps 2–3 were a presence sentinel until now: ``Hypothesis.locked``
6	asserted only that ``locked_at`` and ``timestamp_proof`` were non-``None``, so a hand-written
7	``timestamp_proof="rfc3161:demo"`` "locked" a hypothesis and nothing stopped a study from
8	locking *after* it had seen the baseline, or from swapping the hypothesis content after locking
9	(audit pass 1, issue #6). Both firewalls (:mod:`adjudication`, :mod:`discovery`) and all of
10	:mod:`confirm` rest on the lock, so a sentinel lock made the three verdicts decorative.
11	
12	This module makes the lock mean what the method says, by construction:
13	
14	- **Content binding** — :func:`canonical_hypothesis_digest` is a type-faithful SHA-256 over a
15	  hypothesis's *decision-bearing* fields (statement, kind, origin, test, decision rule,
16	  predicted direction, source claim, params). A valid proof must attest to *this* digest, so
17	  the content the verdict is about is provably the content that was locked: change the claim,
18	  the test, or the decision rule after locking and verification fails.
19	- **Verifiable timestamp** — a pluggable :class:`TimestampAuthority` returns a
20	  :class:`VerifiedTimestamp` only if the proof genuinely binds the digest. The engine ships one
21	  real, data-sovereign authority (:class:`LocalHmacAuthority`, on-box HMAC) and the *Protocol*
22	  so a study can plug an RFC-3161 TSA. It ships **no** silent-accept authority.
23	- **Lock before confirm** — :func:`require_preregistered` rejects a lock whose attested time is
24	  not strictly before the confirmation moment, so a hypothesis cannot be "pre"-registered after
25	  the result is known.
26	
27	Honest scope (the lesson carried from ADR-0008): :class:`LocalHmacAuthority` proves content
28	integrity, time binding, and unforgeability *relative to a secret that lives on this box*.
29	Whoever holds that secret could backdate a proof — it is local tamper-evidence, **not**
30	third-party non-repudiation. A study that needs non-repudiation wires an RFC-3161 authority
31	(which sends only the digest, never the data, so it stays compatible with data sovereignty —
32	ADR-0003 — at the cost of an external dependency the study opts into). And content binding
33	covers the *structured fields*: free text inside ``statement``/``params`` is hashed (so it
34	cannot change after locking) but the engine cannot judge whether that text is *meaningful* —
35	that remains a review obligation (GOVERNANCE §2 "timestamped adaptive design").
36	"""
37	
38	from __future__ import annotations
39	
40	import datetime as _dt
41	import hmac
42	from dataclasses import dataclass, replace
43	from typing import Protocol
44	
45	from assay_engine._canonical import canonical_json, hash_text
46	from assay_engine.methodology.firewalls import FirewallViolation
47	from assay_engine.methodology.hypothesis import Hypothesis
48	
49	_UTC = _dt.timezone.utc
50	_PROOF_PREFIX = "local-hmac:v1:"
51	_PROOF_FIELD_SEP = "|"  # ISO-8601 and lowercase hex never contain "|"
52	
53	
54	class PreRegistrationError(FirewallViolation):
55	    """A hypothesis failed pre-registration verification (METHODOLOGY §3, GOVERNANCE §2).
56	
57	    A subclass of :class:`FirewallViolation` so existing ``except FirewallViolation`` handlers
58	    in the runners and studies treat a bad lock as the firewall breach it is.
59	    """
60	
61	
62	@dataclass(frozen=True, slots=True)
63	class VerifiedTimestamp:
64	    """The authority's attestation that ``digest`` existed at ``instant``."""
65	
66	    instant: _dt.datetime  # timezone-aware, normalized to UTC
67	    authority: str
68	    digest: str
69	
70	
71	class TimestampAuthority(Protocol):
72	    """Engine contract: verify that ``proof`` binds ``digest``, returning the attested time.
73	
74	    Implementations MUST raise :class:`PreRegistrationError` (not return a sentinel) when the
75	    proof is malformed, does not bind ``digest``, or is otherwise invalid — the engine treats a
76	    non-raising return as a genuine attestation.
77	    """
78	
79	    def verify(self, digest: str, proof: str) -> VerifiedTimestamp: ...
80	
81	
82	class StampingAuthority(TimestampAuthority, Protocol):
83	    """A study-side authority that can also *issue* proofs (used by :func:`lock_hypothesis`)."""
84	
85	    def stamp(self, digest: str, *, instant: _dt.datetime | None = None) -> str: ...
86	
87	
88	def _require_aware_utc(instant: _dt.datetime, what: str) -> _dt.datetime:
89	    if instant.tzinfo is None or instant.utcoffset() is None:
90	        raise PreRegistrationError(
91	            f"{what} must be timezone-aware (a naive datetime has no defined instant)"
92	        )
93	    return instant.astimezone(_UTC)
94	
95	
96	def canonical_hypothesis_digest(hypothesis: Hypothesis) -> str:
97	    """A type-faithful SHA-256 over a hypothesis's decision-bearing content **and its id**.
98	
99	    Includes ``hypothesis_id``: although it is a label, it is fixed at lock time and is the key
100	    the runners attribute verdicts by (``verdict.hypothesis_id``), so leaving it unbound would
101	    let a post-lock id swap file a verdict under an id the proof never attested (adversarial
102	    review, #80). Excludes only the lock fields themselves (``locked_at``/``timestamp_proof`` —
103	    they attest *to* this digest, so binding them would be circular).
104	
105	    Raises :class:`PreRegistrationError` if the content cannot be canonicalized reproducibly
106	    (a non-finite float or an unhashable leaf in ``params``) — the pre-registration entrypoints
107	    must surface a typed firewall error, not a bare ``ValueError``/``TypeError`` (#82).
108	    """
109	    payload = {
110	        "hypothesis_id": hypothesis.hypothesis_id,
111	        "statement": hypothesis.statement,
112	        "kind": hypothesis.kind.value,
113	        "origin": hypothesis.origin.value,
114	        "test_name": hypothesis.test_name,
115	        "decision_rule": hypothesis.decision_rule,
116	        "source_claim_id": hypothesis.source_claim_id,
117	        "predicted_direction": hypothesis.predicted_direction,
118	        "params": dict(hypothesis.params),
119	    }
120	    try:
121	        return hash_text(canonical_json(payload))
122	    except (ValueError, TypeError) as exc:
123	        raise PreRegistrationError(
124	            f"hypothesis {hypothesis.hypothesis_id!r} cannot be canonicalized for a content "
125	            f"digest ({exc}); pre-registration requires reproducibly hashable content "
126	            "(no non-finite floats or unhashable leaves in params)"
127	        ) from None
128	
129	
130	class LocalHmacAuthority:
131	    """A real, on-box timestamp authority binding a digest and an instant under a secret key.
132	
133	    ``stamp`` issues ``local-hmac:v1:<instant-iso>|<hmac-hex>`` where the MAC is
134	    ``HMAC-SHA256(secret, digest || instant)``. ``verify`` recomputes the MAC over the digest
135	    the engine derived from the *current* hypothesis and the instant carried in the proof, and
136	    constant-time compares. The proof therefore verifies iff the content is unchanged, the
137	    instant is unchanged, and the same secret is used — without the secret it cannot be forged
138	    for arbitrary content or time.
139	
140	    Honest scope: this is local tamper-evidence, not third-party non-repudiation — the holder of
141	    ``secret`` could backdate (see module docstring). Use an RFC-3161 authority when you need a
142	    third party to vouch for the time.
143	    """
144	
145	    authority_id = "local-hmac"
146	
147	    def __init__(self, secret: bytes) -> None:
148	        if not isinstance(secret, (bytes, bytearray)) or len(secret) < 16:
149	            raise ValueError("LocalHmacAuthority secret must be >= 16 bytes of key material")
150	        self._secret = bytes(secret)
151	
152	    def _mac(self, digest: str, instant_iso: str) -> str:
153	        msg = canonical_json(["local-hmac:v1", digest, instant_iso]).encode("utf-8")
154	        return hmac.new(self._secret, msg, "sha256").hexdigest()
155	
156	    def stamp(self, digest: str, *, instant: _dt.datetime | None = None) -> str:
157	        when = instant if instant is not None else _dt.datetime.now(tz=_UTC)
158	        when = _require_aware_utc(when, "stamp instant")
159	        instant_iso = when.isoformat()
160	        return f"{_PROOF_PREFIX}{instant_iso}{_PROOF_FIELD_SEP}{self._mac(digest, instant_iso)}"
161	
162	    def verify(self, digest: str, proof: str) -> VerifiedTimestamp:
163	        if not proof.startswith(_PROOF_PREFIX):
164	            raise PreRegistrationError(
165	                f"proof is not a {self.authority_id} v1 token (wrong prefix)"
166	            )
167	        body = proof[len(_PROOF_PREFIX) :]
168	        instant_iso, sep, mac_hex = body.partition(_PROOF_FIELD_SEP)
169	        if not sep or not instant_iso or not mac_hex:
170	            raise PreRegistrationError("malformed local-hmac proof (expected <instant>|<mac>)")
171	        # The MAC field must be exactly the hex of a SHA-256 digest. Validating its shape here
172	        # (a) rejects garbage early and (b) keeps non-ASCII/non-hex out of hmac.compare_digest,
173	        # which raises TypeError on non-ASCII str rather than returning False (#81).
174	        if len(mac_hex) != 64 or any(c not in "0123456789abcdef" for c in mac_hex):
175	            raise PreRegistrationError("malformed local-hmac proof (mac is not 64 lowercase hex)")
176	        try:
177	            parsed = _dt.datetime.fromisoformat(instant_iso)
178	        except ValueError:
179	            raise PreRegistrationError(f"proof instant is not ISO-8601: {instant_iso!r}") from None
180	        instant = _require_aware_utc(parsed, "proof instant")
181	        expected = self._mac(digest, instant_iso)
182	        if not hmac.compare_digest(expected, mac_hex):
183	            # Either the content (digest) changed since locking, the instant was tampered, or the
184	            # proof was issued under a different secret. All are pre-registration failures.
185	            raise PreRegistrationError(
186	                "proof does not bind this hypothesis's content at the stated time "
187	                "(content changed after locking, or proof was forged/issued by another authority)"
188	            )
189	        return VerifiedTimestamp(instant=instant, authority=self.authority_id, digest=digest)
190	
191	
192	def lock_hypothesis(
193	    hypothesis: Hypothesis,
194	    *,
195	    authority: StampingAuthority,
196	    instant: _dt.datetime | None = None,
197	) -> Hypothesis:
198	    """Return a locked copy of ``hypothesis`` whose proof binds its current content.
199	
200	    The engine's blessed way to pre-register: it computes the content digest, has ``authority``
201	    stamp it, and records the attested instant in ``locked_at`` so the displayed lock time and
202	    the cryptographic proof always agree. Re-locking an already-locked hypothesis is refused.
203	
204	    Scope of the seal (same honest boundary as ADR-0008): this refuses re-locking *this object*.
205	    It cannot stop a study that willfully reconstructs a hypothesis — e.g. ``dataclasses.replace``
206	    to clear the lock fields, then re-lock different content — any more than the runners can stop
207	    a builder that reflects into their frame; both need process isolation, which is out of scope.
208	    What the engine *does* guarantee is that whatever is ultimately confirmed has a proof binding
209	    its content at an attested time before confirmation. So a re-locked copy is simply a new
210	    pre-registration with a new, later timestamp — visible as such, not a silent override.
211	    """
212	    if hypothesis.locked:
213	        raise PreRegistrationError(
214	            f"hypothesis {hypothesis.hypothesis_id!r} is already locked; re-locking is forbidden"
215	        )
216	    digest = canonical_hypothesis_digest(hypothesis)
217	    proof = authority.stamp(digest, instant=instant)
218	    verified = authority.verify(digest, proof)  # fail loud here if the authority is misbehaving
219	    return replace(hypothesis, locked_at=verified.instant.isoformat(), timestamp_proof=proof)
220	
221	
222	def verify_preregistration(
223	    hypothesis: Hypothesis, *, authority: TimestampAuthority
224	) -> VerifiedTimestamp:
225	    """Verify a hypothesis is genuinely pre-registered: present, content-bound, time-attested.
226	
227	    Raises :class:`PreRegistrationError` if the hypothesis is not locked, the proof does not
228	    bind the *current* content digest, the attested instant is not a timezone-aware time, or the
229	    self-reported ``locked_at`` disagrees with the authority's attested instant.
230	    """
231	    if not hypothesis.locked:
232	        raise PreRegistrationError(
233	            f"hypothesis {hypothesis.hypothesis_id!r} is not locked; confirm is forbidden "
234	            "before pre-registration (METHODOLOGY §3 / GOVERNANCE §2)"
235	        )
236	    assert hypothesis.timestamp_proof is not None  # .locked guarantees this; for the type checker
237	    digest = canonical_hypothesis_digest(hypothesis)
238	    verified = authority.verify(digest, hypothesis.timestamp_proof)
239	    if verified.digest != digest:
240	        raise PreRegistrationError(
241	            f"authority attested digest {verified.digest!r} but the hypothesis content hashes to "
242	            f"{digest!r} — the proof does not cover this hypothesis"
243	        )
244	    # The displayed lock time must match the attested one, so a study cannot show a time the
245	    # authority did not vouch for.
246	    try:
247	        claimed = _dt.datetime.fromisoformat(hypothesis.locked_at or "")
248	    except ValueError:
249	        raise PreRegistrationError(
250	            f"locked_at {hypothesis.locked_at!r} is not an ISO-8601 timestamp"
251	        ) from None
252	    if _require_aware_utc(claimed, "locked_at") != verified.instant:
253	        raise PreRegistrationError(
254	            f"locked_at {hypothesis.locked_at!r} disagrees with the authority-attested instant "
255	            f"{verified.instant.isoformat()!r}"
256	        )
257	    return verified
258	
259	
260	def require_preregistered(
261	    hypothesis: Hypothesis,
262	    *,
263	    authority: TimestampAuthority,
264	    not_after: _dt.datetime,
265	) -> VerifiedTimestamp:
266	    """Gate a confirmatory step: the hypothesis must be verifiably locked *before* ``not_after``.
267	
268	    ``not_after`` is the confirmation moment (a runner captures it at entry). A lock whose
269	    attested instant is not strictly earlier is rejected — you cannot pre-register a hypothesis
270	    after the result that would confirm it is already in hand.
271	    """
272	    not_after = _require_aware_utc(not_after, "not_after")
273	    verified = verify_preregistration(hypothesis, authority=authority)
274	    if not verified.instant < not_after:
275	        raise PreRegistrationError(
276	            f"hypothesis {hypothesis.hypothesis_id!r} was locked at "
277	            f"{verified.instant.isoformat()!r}, not strictly before the confirmation moment "
278	            f"{not_after.isoformat()!r} — a lock must precede confirmation (GOVERNANCE §2)"
279	        )
280	    return verified
281

1	"""Typed, falsifiable hypotheses (METHODOLOGY.md §3).
2	
3	Every hypothesis names what is claimed, the test, the data it runs on, and a pre-specified
4	decision rule. Two origins:
5	
6	- ``DISCOVERY`` — surfaced by the data, then made specific, locked, and timestamped before
7	  confirmation (Firewall B).
8	- ``EXTERNAL_CLAIM`` — derived by the adapter from an external expert claim, tested against
9	  the blind baseline (Firewall A).
10	
11	Two kinds, which select the confirmation mechanism:
12	
13	- ``UNIT_LEVEL`` — confirmed on a held-out split.
14	- ``WHOLE_CORPUS`` — no held-out object possible; confirmed against null/permutation
15	  distributions and stability across resamples.
16	"""
17	
18	from __future__ import annotations
19	
20	from dataclasses import dataclass, field
21	from enum import Enum
22	from typing import Any, Literal, Mapping
23	
24	from assay_engine._frozen import freeze_mapping
25	
26	# The tail a claim predicts the statistic lies in, relative to the null.
27	Direction = Literal["greater", "less"]
28	
29	
30	class HypothesisKind(Enum):
31	    UNIT_LEVEL = "unit_level"
32	    WHOLE_CORPUS = "whole_corpus"
33	
34	
35	class HypothesisOrigin(Enum):
36	    DISCOVERY = "discovery"
37	    EXTERNAL_CLAIM = "external_claim"
38	
39	
40	@dataclass(frozen=True, slots=True)
41	class Hypothesis:
42	    """A locked, falsifiable assertion ready for confirmatory testing.
43	
44	    ``locked_at`` and ``timestamp_proof`` are populated when the hypothesis is sealed for
45	    pre-registration. An unlocked hypothesis (either field ``None``) must not be passed to a
46	    confirmatory test.
47	
48	    Scope of ``locked`` (audit pass 1, issue #6): ``locked`` is only a cheap **presence**
49	    predicate — both fields populated. It is NOT the methodology-grade check and carries no
50	    cryptographic meaning on its own. The real pre-registration verification —
51	    content-binding (the proof covers this hypothesis's content), timestamp attestation, and
52	    lock-before-confirm ordering — lives in
53	    :mod:`assay_engine.methodology.preregistration` (``verify_preregistration`` /
54	    ``require_preregistered``), and the confirmatory runners (:mod:`adjudication`,
55	    :mod:`discovery`) enforce it via a supplied ``TimestampAuthority``. Use
56	    :func:`~assay_engine.methodology.preregistration.lock_hypothesis` to produce a hypothesis
57	    whose lock actually verifies; a hand-set ``timestamp_proof`` string satisfies ``locked``
58	    but will fail ``verify_preregistration``.
59	    """
60	
61	    hypothesis_id: str
62	    statement: str
63	    kind: HypothesisKind
64	    origin: HypothesisOrigin
65	    test_name: str
66	    decision_rule: str
67	    source_claim_id: str | None = None
68	    locked_at: str | None = None
69	    timestamp_proof: str | None = None
70	    # The predicted direction, fixed at pre-registration so it cannot be chosen post-hoc
71	    # after seeing the baseline (audit pass 2, issue #24). For an EXTERNAL_CLAIM hypothesis
72	    # the adapter derives this from the claim's assertion, not from the baseline.
73	    predicted_direction: Direction | None = None
74	    params: Mapping[str, Any] = field(default_factory=dict)
75	
76	    @property
77	    def locked(self) -> bool:
78	        return self.locked_at is not None and self.timestamp_proof is not None
79	
80	    def __post_init__(self) -> None:
81	        if self.origin is HypothesisOrigin.EXTERNAL_CLAIM and not self.source_claim_id:
82	            raise ValueError("EXTERNAL_CLAIM hypotheses must carry a source_claim_id")
83	        # Validate predicted_direction at the earliest possible point (pass 3, #F-002): an
84	        # invalid tail ("up", "UP", "") must never reach lock_hypothesis (which would
85	        # cryptographically bind garbage into the proof) nor confirm_unit_level (which does not
86	        # otherwise re-validate it). Only the two recognized tails — or None (deferred to
87	        # confirm time on the whole-corpus path) — are admissible.
88	        if self.predicted_direction is not None and self.predicted_direction not in (
89	            "greater",
90	            "less",
91	        ):
92	            raise ValueError(
93	                "predicted_direction must be 'greater' or 'less' (or None); got "
94	                f"{self.predicted_direction!r} — an unrecognized tail would silently flip the "
95	                "supported/contradicted verdict"
96	            )
97	        object.__setattr__(self, "params", freeze_mapping(self.params))
98

content.text: Now let me read the whole-corpus confirmer (lines 210-397) to see how alpha and stability_threshold are used there.

content.content:
210	def _resample_stability(
211	    resamples: Sequence[float], null: Sequence[float], tail: Direction, alpha: float
212	) -> float:
213	    """Fraction of resamples that **reproduce the observed effect**.
214	
215	    Each resample is scored as its own observed statistic against the null and must itself be
216	    significant in the predicted tail (empirical p ≤ ``alpha``). This measures reproduction of
217	    the observed *effect* — not merely landing on the predicted side of the null center, which a
218	    resample distribution clearing the null median by an epsilon would satisfy while reproducing
219	    nothing of the observed magnitude (audit pass 2, #139). The closer to 1.0, the more
220	    consistently the effect's significance reproduces across resamples.
221	
222	    The default path is O(R·N) pure Python (one null pass per resample). When numpy is available
223	    (the optional ``baseline`` extra) the whole R×N comparison runs as a single vectorized op
224	    (pass 3, #F-017). The numpy path computes the IDENTICAL integer extreme-counts and the same
225	    ``(extreme + 1) / (n + 1) <= alpha`` test, so its result is exactly equal to the Python path;
226	    it is purely a speed optimization for large R·N, never a semantic change.
227	    """
228	    n = len(null)
229	    try:
230	        import numpy as np
231	
232	        # Use searchsorted on a sorted null (O((R+N) log N) time, O(R) memory) rather than the
233	        # R×N broadcast boolean matrix (#G-010): the matrix peaks at ~R*N bytes (250MB for
234	        # R=5000,N=50000) and can OOM where the pure-Python loop would not. The integer
235	        # extreme-counts are IDENTICAL to _empirical_p: for 'greater', count(null >= r) = N -
236	        # searchsorted(sorted, r, 'left'); for 'less', count(null <= r) = searchsorted(sorted, r,
237	        # 'right'). Then (extreme+1)/(n+1) <= alpha, same as the pure path.
238	        sorted_null = np.sort(np.asarray(null, dtype=np.float64))
239	        res_arr = np.asarray(resamples, dtype=np.float64)
240	        if tail == "greater":
241	            extreme = n - np.searchsorted(sorted_null, res_arr, side="left")
242	        else:
243	            extreme = np.searchsorted(sorted_null, res_arr, side="right")
244	        p = (extreme + 1) / (n + 1)
245	        return float(int((p <= alpha).sum()) / len(resamples))
246	    except ImportError:
247	        significant = sum(1 for r in resamples if _empirical_p(null, r, tail) <= alpha)
248	        return significant / len(resamples)
249	
250	
251	_VALID_DIRECTIONS = ("greater", "less")
252	
253	
254	def _resolve_direction(hypothesis: Hypothesis, predicted_direction: Direction | None) -> Direction:
255	    """Direction is fixed at lock time on the hypothesis (issue #24); the confirm-time argument is
256	    optional and, if given, must MATCH the pre-registered one — it can cross-check, never supply.
257	
258	    A hypothesis reaching here has already passed the confirm gate, so it is locked and its
259	    ``predicted_direction`` is part of the cryptographic binding. A ``None`` locked direction
260	    therefore means the tail was *not* pre-registered; accepting a confirm-time direction in that
261	    case would let the analyst pick the tail AFTER seeing the data (HARKing), flipping
262	    supported↔contradicted on a hypothesis whose lock attests "no direction" (audit pass 4,
263	    #G-001). The tail must be committed before confirmation, so a directionless locked hypothesis
264	    is refused outright rather than completed with a post-hoc direction.
265	
266	    The resolved direction is validated against ``{"greater", "less"}``; any other value is
267	    rejected (an unrecognized tail would silently fall through to 'less' in :func:`_empirical_p`,
268	    flipping the verdict — audit pass 2, #128).
269	    """
270	    locked_dir = hypothesis.predicted_direction
271	    if locked_dir is None:
272	        raise ValueError(
273	            "predicted_direction was not pre-registered: a whole-corpus confirmatory hypothesis "
274	            "must lock its tail ('greater' or 'less') BEFORE confirmation. Supplying a confirm-time "
275	            "direction for a directionless locked hypothesis selects the tail after seeing the data "
276	            "(HARKing) and is refused (#G-001)."
277	        )
278	    if predicted_direction is not None and predicted_direction != locked_dir:
279	        raise ValueError(
280	            "predicted_direction argument contradicts the hypothesis's pre-registered "
281	            f"direction ({predicted_direction!r} != {locked_dir!r})"
282	        )
283	    resolved = locked_dir
284	    if resolved not in _VALID_DIRECTIONS:
285	        raise ValueError(
286	            f"predicted_direction must be one of {_VALID_DIRECTIONS}; got {resolved!r} — an "
287	            "unrecognized tail would silently flip the supported/contradicted verdict"
288	        )
289	    return resolved
290	
291	
292	def confirm_whole_corpus(
293	    hypothesis: Hypothesis,
294	    *,
295	    observed: float,
296	    null_distribution: Sequence[float],
297	    alpha: float,
298	    resample_statistics: Sequence[float] | None = None,
299	    stability_threshold: float = 0.9,
300	    predicted_direction: Direction | None = None,
301	    authority: TimestampAuthority | None = None,
302	) -> Verdict:
303	    """Confirm a whole-corpus hypothesis against a null distribution + measured stability.
304	
305	    Pass ``authority`` to verify pre-registration in full (content binding + timestamp +
306	    lock-before-now); omit it to fall back to the presence gate (see :func:`require_locked`).
307	
308	    The predicted tail is taken from the hypothesis's pre-registered ``predicted_direction``
309	    (issue #24). The pattern must (a) beat the null at ``alpha`` in the predicted tail and
310	    (b) be **stable across resamples** — measured by the engine from ``resample_statistics``,
311	    not asserted by the caller (audit pass 2, issue #21). Outcomes:
312	
313	    - significant in the predicted tail AND stable → ``supported``;
314	    - significant in the *opposite* tail → ``contradicted``;
315	    - unstable, not significant, or stability not assessed → ``indeterminate``.
316	
317	    If ``resample_statistics`` is omitted, stability cannot be measured, so ``supported`` is
318	    unavailable and the verdict is ``indeterminate`` ("stability not assessed").
319	    """
320	    if hypothesis.kind is not HypothesisKind.WHOLE_CORPUS:
321	        raise ValueError("confirm_whole_corpus requires a WHOLE_CORPUS hypothesis")
322	    _gate_preregistration(hypothesis, authority)
323	    _validate_alpha(alpha)
324	    _validate_finite("observed", observed)
325	    if not null_distribution:
326	        raise ValueError(
327	            "a null/permutation distribution is required for whole-corpus confirmation"
328	        )
329	    for v in null_distribution:
330	        _validate_finite("null_distribution value", v)
331	    if not (0.0 < stability_threshold <= 1.0):
332	        raise ValueError(f"stability_threshold must be in (0, 1]; got {stability_threshold}")
333	
334	    direction = _resolve_direction(hypothesis, predicted_direction)
335	    opposite: Direction = "less" if direction == "greater" else "greater"
336	    p_support = _empirical_p(null_distribution, observed, direction)
337	    p_contra = _empirical_p(null_distribution, observed, opposite)
338	    n = len(null_distribution)
339	
340	    stability: float | None = None
341	    contra_stability: float | None = None
342	    if resample_statistics is not None:
343	        if not resample_statistics:
344	            raise ValueError("resample_statistics, if given, must be non-empty")
345	        for r in resample_statistics:
346	            _validate_finite("resample statistic", r)
347	        stability = _resample_stability(resample_statistics, null_distribution, direction, alpha)
348	        # Also measure how consistently the resamples reproduce the OPPOSITE-tail significance, so a
349	        # contradiction's robustness is recorded in evidence even though (by design) a contradiction
350	        # is reportable without it (#G-005).
351	        contra_stability = _resample_stability(
352	            resample_statistics, null_distribution, opposite, alpha
353	        )
354	    stable = stability is not None and stability >= stability_threshold
355	
356	    rule = (
357	        f"empirical p from {n}-sample null at alpha={alpha}, predicted tail '{direction}'; "
358	        f"stability across resamples >= {stability_threshold} (engine-measured)"
359	    )
360	    evidence = {
361	        "p_support": p_support,
362	        "p_contradict": p_contra,
363	        "null_n": n,
364	        "predicted_direction": direction,
365	        "stability": stability,
366	        "contradiction_stability": contra_stability,
367	        "stability_threshold": stability_threshold,
368	        "n_resamples": len(resample_statistics) if resample_statistics is not None else 0,
369	    }
370	    common = dict(statistic=observed, threshold=alpha, evidence=evidence)
371	
372	    # A contradiction (significant in the OPPOSITE tail) is reportable without a stability gate, and
373	    # this asymmetry with `supported` is INTENTIONAL (#G-005): support is a positive claim that must
374	    # reproduce to be decisive, whereas a statistic landing significantly in the wrong tail is
375	    # evidence OF the opposite — not absence of evidence — so it stands on the observed effect.
376	    # `contradiction_stability` is recorded in evidence (when resamples were supplied) so an auditor
377	    # can see whether the contradiction also reproduces, even though it is not gated on it.
378	    if p_contra <= alpha:
379	        return Verdict.contradicted(
380	            hypothesis.hypothesis_id, rule, notes="significant in the opposite tail", **common
381	        )
382	    if stability is None:
383	        return Verdict.indeterminate(
384	            hypothesis.hypothesis_id,
385	            rule,
386	            notes="stability not assessed (no resamples supplied)",
387	            **common,
388	        )
389	    if not stable:
390	        return Verdict.indeterminate(
391	            hypothesis.hypothesis_id, rule, notes="unstable across resamples", **common
392	        )
393	    if p_support <= alpha:
394	        return Verdict.supported(hypothesis.hypothesis_id, rule, **common)
395	    return Verdict.indeterminate(
396	        hypothesis.hypothesis_id, rule, notes="does not beat null at alpha", **common
397	    )
398

content.text:
The finding is confirmed by the code. `alpha` and `stability_threshold` are runtime arguments to `confirm_whole_corpus` (lines 297, 299) and `confirm_unit_level` (line 153), and are NOT fields on `Hypothesis` nor in `canonical_hypothesis_digest` (lines 109-119). The contrast with `_resolve_direction` (lines 254-289), which DOES read the locked value and cross-checks any argument, makes the gap stark.

Let me verify the PoC reproduces by writing a test against the actual code.

input.command:
cd /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay && python3 -c "
import datetime as dt
from assay_engine.methodology.hypothesis import Hypothesis, HypothesisKind, HypothesisOrigin
from assay_engine.methodology.preregistration import lock_hypothesis, LocalHmacAuthority
from assay_engine.methodology.confirm import confirm_whole_corpus

auth = LocalHmacAuthority(b'0123456789abcdef0123456789abcdef')
h = Hypothesis(
    hypothesis_id='h1',
    statement='effect is greater than null',
    kind=HypothesisKind.WHOLE_CORPUS,
    origin=HypothesisOrigin.DISCOVERY,
    test_name='perm',
    decision_rule='beats null at alpha',
    predicted_direction='greater',
)
# lock BEFORE confirmation, in the past
h = lock_hypothesis(h, authority=auth, instant=dt.datetime(2020,1,1,tzinfo=dt.timezone.utc))

# construct a null and observed where verdict is threshold-sensitive
null = [float(i) for i in range(100)]   # 0..99
observed = 92.0  # p_support = (#>=92 +1)/101 = (8+1)/101 = 0.089
# resamples that reproduce significance ~70% of the time
resamples = [95.0]*7 + [50.0]*3  # 7/10 significant at alpha=0.10, fewer at 0.05

v1 = confirm_whole_corpus(h, observed=observed, null_distribution=null, alpha=0.05,
                          resample_statistics=resamples, stability_threshold=0.9, authority=auth)
v2 = confirm_whole_corpus(h, observed=observed, null_distribution=null, alpha=0.10,
                          resample_statistics=resamples, stability_threshold=0.6, authority=auth)
print('SAME locked hypothesis, digest:', h.timestamp_proof[:40])
print('alpha=0.05, stab_thr=0.9 ->', v1.status if hasattr(v1,'status') else v1)
print('alpha=0.10, stab_thr=0.6 ->', v2.status if hasattr(v2,'status') else v2)
print('verdict objects:', v1, v2)
"

v2 (alpha=0.10, thr=0.6): Verdict(hypothesis_id='h1', label=<VerdictLabel.SUPPORTED: 'supported'>, decision_rule="empirical p from 100-sample null at alpha=0.1, predicted tail 'greater'; stability across resamples >= 0.6 (engine-measured)", statistic=92.0, threshold=0.1, evidence=FrozenDict({'p_support': 0.0891089108910891, 'p_contradict': 0.9306930693069307, 'null_n': 100, 'predicted_direction': 'greater', 'stability': 0.7, 'contradiction_stability': 0.0, 'stability_threshold': 0.6, 'n_resamples': 10}), notes='') | rule: empirical p from 100-sample null at alpha=0.1, predicted tail 'greater'; stability across resamples >= 0.6 (engine-measured)
proof unchanged (same lock): local-hmac:v1:2020-01-01T00:00

content.text:
The PoC reproduces exactly. The same locked hypothesis (identical proof, passing pre-registration), confirmed twice:
- alpha=0.05, threshold=0.9 → **INDETERMINATE** ("unstable across resamples")
- alpha=0.10, threshold=0.6 → **SUPPORTED**

The verdict flipped purely by post-hoc threshold choice, and the recorded `decision_rule` was constructed from the runtime alpha (line 356-359) rather than checked against the locked rule. This is exactly the threshold-HARKing the firewall claims to prevent.

The finding is genuine and grounded in current code (4 passes applied). The contrast with `_resolve_direction` (which the same pass-4 work hardened against post-hoc direction choice, #G-001) shows the authors knew this class of attack but left alpha/stability_threshold unguarded.

input.reasoning:
Confirmed in current code and the PoC reproduces against the actual engine.

Evidence:
1. Hypothesis (src/assay_engine/methodology/hypothesis.py:61-74) has NO alpha or stability_threshold field.
2. canonical_hypothesis_digest (preregistration.py:109-119) hashes statement/kind/origin/test_name/decision_rule/source_claim_id/predicted_direction/params/hypothesis_id only — neither threshold is bound by the pre-registration proof.
3. alpha and stability_threshold are accepted as plain runtime arguments at confirm time: confirm_whole_corpus (confirm.py:297, 299), confirm_unit_level (confirm.py:153), verdict_from_pvalue (confirm.py:106). Nothing reads hypothesis.decision_rule or hypothesis.params to constrain them.
4. The recorded decision_rule is CONSTRUCTED from the runtime alpha rather than compared to the locked rule: confirm.py:131 (verdict_from_pvalue) and confirm.py:356-359 (confirm_whole_corpus). So the audit record reflects the post-hoc threshold, not a committed one.
5. This is in stark, deliberate contrast to _resolve_direction (confirm.py:254-289), which reads the LOCKED predicted_direction and rejects any contradicting confirm-time argument (#G-001, pass 4) — proving the authors hardened exactly this HARKing class for direction but left the two most decision-bearing thresholds unguarded.

PoC executed against the real engine (.venv/bin/python, Python 3.14): a single WHOLE_CORPUS hypothesis locked via lock_hypothesis with predicted_direction='greater', null=range(100), observed=92.0, resamples=[95]*7+[50]*3.
- confirm_whole_corpus(..., alpha=0.05, stability_threshold=0.9) -> INDETERMINATE ('unstable across resamples')
- confirm_whole_corpus(..., alpha=0.10, stability_threshold=0.6) -> SUPPORTED
Same locked hypothesis, identical timestamp_proof, both pass require_preregistered. Verdict flipped from indeterminate to supported purely by post-hoc threshold selection after the null/baseline is visible — precisely the threshold-HARKing the pre-registration firewall claims to prevent.

Not speculative, not by-design (the module docstrings and #G-001 fix establish that committing decision-bearing numbers before confirmation IS the intended guarantee), and not already-handled. High rather than critical because exploitation requires an analyst with confirm-time control (an internal/methodological integrity threat, not a remote attacker) and the lock itself is otherwise sound; but it defeats the core methodological guarantee of the system, so it is a true production-readiness problem.

content.text:
Confirmed H-001 as a genuine high-severity production-readiness problem.

Key file:line evidence:
- `src/assay_engine/methodology/hypothesis.py:61-74` — no `alpha`/`stability_threshold` fields
- `src/assay_engine/methodology/preregistration.py:109-119` — digest excludes both thresholds
- `src/assay_engine/methodology/confirm.py:297,299` (whole-corpus), `:153` (unit-level), `:106` (verdict_from_pvalue) — thresholds taken as runtime args
- `src/assay_engine/methodology/confirm.py:131` and `:356-359` — recorded `decision_rule` built from runtime alpha, not checked against the locked rule
- `src/assay_engine/methodology/confirm.py:254-289` (`_resolve_direction`) — the contrasting hardened path that does read/cross-check the locked value (#G-001), proving the gap is an oversight not by-design

The PoC reproduced live: the same locked hypothesis flips INDETERMINATE → SUPPORTED purely by changing confirm-time alpha (0.05→0.10) and stability_threshold (0.9→0.6), with the proof unchanged and pre-registration still passing.
